purpose: Reconcile B2C-SHIP-014 (dedup broken) vs SF-020 (dedup works) contradiction
note: Protocol adapted: storefront UI path for THIS user (org-affiliated) adds COMPANY addresses (org-level, memberId=BMW-Group orgId), NOT contact addresses. Path A tested contact. Added Path A2 testing org-level via same GraphQL mutation to match what B2C-SHIP-014 actually hits.

pathA_graphql_contact.observation: Contact-level currentCustomerAddresses = 4 before + after. No new row. Existing id 672cc35e unchanged.

pathA2_graphql_organization.observation: Organization-level updateMemberAddresses INSERTED a duplicate (totalCount 10 → 11). 3 prior identical rows already existed. New row has name=null while existing dups have auto-computed name='IND, Delhi, deli bumbaoo 453'.

pathB_storefront_ui.reason: Org-affiliated Elena Mutykova sees 'Ship to:' header dropdown that opens 'Create a new company address' dialog. This dialog hits updateMemberAddresses with ORG memberId — exactly what Path A2 tested directly. Running both would be duplicate; Path A2's network-level evidence is stronger.
pathB_storefront_ui.note: The 'Addresses' sidebar link is hidden for org users (per project memory). No contact-level storefront UI exists for this account to create a personal address.

verdict: MIXED — server dedupes on Contact memberId, server does NOT dedupe on Organization memberId
reconcilesWith: B2C-SHIP-014 was CORRECT for org-level ship-to addresses (dedup broken). SF-020's claim of 'silent dedupe on computed name key' was PARTIALLY correct for contact-level but WRONG for organization-level which is what the storefront UI creates on this user.

nameFieldAnalysis.finding: The 'name' field is SERVER-COMPUTED from address components (format: 'COUNTRY, City, line1' or 'COUNTRY, Region, City, line1') and stored on the row.
nameFieldAnalysis.hypothesis: If dedup logic uses 'name' as key, it compares incoming (null) with existing (populated) — no match — so insert proceeds. This would explain why orgs accumulate dups over time while contact dedup appears to work (the contact path may use a different key entirely, or benefit from earlier client-side checkDuplicateAddress).
nameFieldAnalysis.contactBehavior: Contact mutation returned totalCount=4 (no insert). Either server dedup works on contact via different key (e.g. composite line1+city+country+postal+type), OR the contact path had a no-op because the submitted address was matched by id implicitly. Either way, contact level does NOT accumulate dups in this test.

corrections: BUG: updateMemberAddresses-Single-Append-Dedup-Miss — KEEP AS FILED but scope tightened to ORG memberType only. Add note: does not reproduce on Contact memberType (totalCount stays constant).

SF-020 'silent dedupe on computed name key' — PARTIALLY CORRECT for contact path; MISLEADING if applied to org path. Update SF-020 to specify it tested Contact-level addresses, and note Org-level behavior differs.

BL-PROFILE-001 (address dedup) — SPLIT into two rules: BL-PROFILE-001a (contact addresses — dedup enforced server-side) and BL-PROFILE-001b (org addresses — dedup NOT enforced; storefront UI creates duplicates silently). Org rule is violated — escalate as P1 data quality issue for B2B accounts.

B2C-SHIP-014 at REG-2026-04-23-2300 (totalCount 4 → 5) was mis-attributed: the '4' is the contact count, but creating via Ship-to UI writes to ORG where totalCount was ~10. The '5' reported in that run likely came from a different query scope or was a misread. Re-run with clarified scope.

cleanupStatus.cleanupMethod: deleteMemberAddresses with full address fields (not just id). Schema requires all InputMemberAddressType fields.
